title: Humboltia chamensis
taxonomy: Humboldtia chamensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family includes a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander family. Within the Apocynaceae family, Humboldtia chamensis is part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The subfamily Rauvolfioideae comprises about 25 genera and is primarily found in tropical regions. This classification reflects the unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships of the plant within its broader botanical context.
uses: Humboltia chamensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayas. It is said to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and roots are often used to make teas and infusions for medicinal purposes. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Humboltia chamensis as a medicinal plant.

side_effects: Humboldtia chamensis is a plant that, like its species, can have side effects due to its toxic compounds. intro: Humboldtia chamensis, also known as the "Cham" tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Humboldtia chamensis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
